results.title: Santa Fe Trail
results.creator_credit: Michael Curtiz, Jack L. Warner and Warner Bros Pictures
results.brief_description: A saga on the American civil war starring Errol Flynn, Olivia de Havilland and Ronald Reagan. Flynn plays Jeb Stuart, a military man who travels in pursuit of John Brown, leader of a violent anti-slavery campaign. Reagan plays George Custer, romantic rival and West Point classmate of Flynn’s. It is an action packed but ambiguous portrayal of the American civil war and its protagonists who held intensively opposing views.

results.title: Blackbeard the pirate
results.creator_credit: Raoul Walsh, Edmund Grainger and RKO Radio Pictures
results.brief_description: Shot in Technicolour, this rousing swashbuckler set in the 17th century tells the story of Blackbeard who, while pillaging the high seas kidnaps the adopted daughter of Sir Henry Morgan, a high official in Jamaica, much to the dismay of her lover, an officer in the British navy. Swordplay and derring-do abound as the timid officer must fight to win back his love, even as Blackbeard duels with his arch-enemy Morgan, who is also a reformed pirate.

results.title: Hondo
results.creator_credit: John Farrow, Robert Fellows and Wayne-Fellows Productions
results.brief_description: Based on the novel by Louis L’Amour, John Wayne’s own favourite movie features the actor in the role of Hondo, a half-Indian cavalry scout. Set in 1874 against the unrest and hostility caused by the white man’s violation of the treaty with the Apache Nation, the plot concerns Hondo’s protection of a young woman and her son whom he discovers living alone in Apache territory. The film extends liberal respect to the Indians and even implicitly endorses the inter-racial romance that develops between the white women and the half-breed Hondo. With impressive cinematography by Robert Burke, the film was restored and re-released by the John Wayne estate in 1995. Cast includes Geraldine Page. Ward Bond and Michael Pate.

results.title: Joan of Arc
results.creator_credit: Victor Fleming, Walter Wanger and Sierra Pictures
results.brief_description: Director Victor Fleming’s final film features Ingrid Bergman as a vivid and luminous Joan of Arc, the 15th-century French peasant girl who led the French in battle against the invading British and was consequently burned at the stake for heresy. Based on the stage play ‘Joan of Lorraine’, by Maxwell Anderson. Cast includes Ingrid Bergman, Jose Ferrer, Ward Bond and Francis L. Sullivan.

results.title: The Left handed gun
results.creator_credit: Arthur Penn, Fred Coe and Warner Bros Pictures
results.brief_description: Adapted from the play by Gore Vidal, “The Left Handed Gun” is the story of William Bonney, the hotheaded gunslinger known as Billy the Kid. Avoiding the usual Hollywood glamourisation of this controversial character, Paul Newman portrays Billy pretty much as he was: an illiterate, homicidal youth. Treated with kindness for the first time in his life by rancher Tunstall, Billy becomes devoted to the rancher. After Tunstall is killed, however, Billy is prompted to go on a murderous spree avenging the death of his former employer. Cast includes: Paul Newman, James Best, Lita Milan, Hurd Hatfield, and James Congdon.

results.title: The Naked truth = Your past is showing
results.creator_credit: Mario Zampi and Rank Organisation Film Productions
results.brief_description: A satire of the tabloid-press industry, “The Naked Truth” focuses on a scandal-sheet publisher, Nigel Dennis, who gets rich by disclosing lurid details of the private lives of wealthy prominent people. Several of his victims attempt to do away with the blackmailing publisher, all to no avail. But when they all join forces, the final plan is brought into play, with each piece slowly falling into place. Cast includes: Dennis Price, Peter Sellers, Terry-Thomas, Shirley Eaton, Peggy Mount, Georgina Cookson, Joan Sims and Miles Malleson.

results.title: D.O.A = DOA
results.creator_credit: Rudolph Mate, Leo Popkin and Cardinal Film
results.brief_description: This fast paced exciting crime story was originally released as a B-movie filler but has, through the years, acquired a reputation as one of the most effective and most impressive of Hollywood film noir. Edmond O’Brien plays an insurance man, unsure about his life and his feelings for his girlfriend Paula (Pamela Britten), who on a weekend trip to San Francisco finds himself the victim of a bizarre poisoning. His attempts to unravel the mystery while counting down the clock to his death lead him to a reappreciation of his own life. Directed by Rudolph Mate who was the cinematographer for Carl Dreyer’s “The Passion of Joan of Arc” and “Vampyr”, the highly evocative cinematography, chilling musical score by Dimitri Tiomkin, and the desperate central performance by O’Brien all contribute to making this a truly suspenseful and dark piece of filmmaking.

results.title: Regeneration = Behind the lines
results.creator_credit: Gilles MacKinnon, Allan Scott, Peter R Simpson, BBC, Norstar Entertainment, Rafford Films, Scottish Arts Council Lottery Fund and Telefilm Canada
results.brief_description: Set in Craiglockhart military hospital during World War I, this is based on a real life encounter between military psychologist Dr William Rivers (Jonathan Pryce), and the poet Siegfried Sassoon (James Wilby) who has been institutionalized in an attempt to undermine his public disapproval of the war. It is also about the young poet Wilfred Owen (Stuart Bunce), who with support from Sassoon, starts to write his great war poems. Gilles MacKinnon’s highly praised adaptation of Pat Barker’s novel is a powerful study of war and its long reaching effects.

results.title: Countdown
results.creator_credit: Robert Altman, William Conrad, Seven Arts Productions and Warner Bros Pictures
results.brief_description: The space-race between the United States and the Soviet Union reaches new heights when the Russians employ plans to send cosmonaughts directly to the moon. In the desperate attempt to be first, NASA sends Lee (James Caan) in an old style Gemini probe on a make-shift mission after three weeks training: to land on the Moon, and then live there for a year until rescued by the Apollo crew. This wry absurdity, directed by Altman after a ten year absence from filmmaking, makes great viewing for it depicts the US government as the “bad guy” and contains several anti-Cold War gestures. Screenplay by Loring Mandel. Based on the book, “Countdown” by Hank Searl.

results.title: Buffalo Bill and the Indians [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: Robert Altman and De Laurentiis Entertainment Group
results.brief_description: Robert Altman’s “Buffalo Bill and the Indians” sardonically explores the gap between western history and legend in show biz-obsessed America. Megalomaniac “Buffalo Bill” Cody assumes the legend created for him by writer Ned Buntline, aided and abetted by his producer and his publicist, perpetuating myths of white triumph over savage “Injuns” in his Wild West show, as audiences cheer him on and buy his merchandise. But when Sitting Bull joins the troupe with his interpreter, his request for authenticity threatens to throw a wrench into the proceedings. Regardless of how Bill may feel about the facts, he must bow to the preferences of the paying public. Cast includes: Paul Newman, Joel Grey, Kevin McCarthy, Harvey Keitel, Allan Nicholls, Geraldine Chaplin, Burt Lancaster, Frank Kaquitts.
